required_skills: Computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulation and analysis for aerospace vehicles
Aerodynamic performance prediction including lift, drag, and pitching moment estimation
Boundary layer theory and turbulence modeling (RANS, LES, DNS)
Transonic, supersonic, and hypersonic aerodynamic flow regime analysis
Wind tunnel test planning, execution, and experimental data reduction
Aerodynamic database (ADB) development and management for flight vehicle programs
Grid generation and mesh refinement strategies for structured and unstructured CFD solvers
Aerothermal heating analysis and thermal protection system (TPS) sizing
Stability and control derivative estimation and aerodynamic coefficient validation
Uncertainty quantification (UQ) and validation and verification (V&V) of CFD results
preferred_skills: Experience with store separation, aeroelasticity, or flutter analysis
Knowledge of design of experiments (DOE) and surrogate modeling for aerodynamic optimization
Familiarity with adjoint-based aerodynamic shape optimization methods
Experience with rotorcraft aerodynamics or propeller/rotor wake modeling
High-performance computing (HPC) cluster job submission and management for large-scale CFD
Knowledge of icing aerodynamics and FAR Part 25 Appendix C certification requirements
Rarefied gas dynamics and direct simulation Monte Carlo (DSMC) methods
responsibilities: Develop and execute high-fidelity CFD simulations using ANSYS Fluent or OpenFOAM to characterize aerodynamic performance across the full flight envelope
Build and maintain vehicle aerodynamic databases (ADB) by integrating CFD predictions and wind tunnel data to support GNC and flight mechanics analyses
Plan and support wind tunnel test campaigns including model design, instrumentation selection, test matrix definition, and post-test data reduction
Perform aerothermal heating analyses for re-entry or high-Mach vehicles to define TPS material requirements and surface temperature margins
Conduct stability and control analysis to compute aerodynamic derivatives and assess vehicle trim, static stability, and dynamic response characteristics
Generate high-quality structured and unstructured CFD meshes using Pointwise or ICEM CFD to resolve boundary layers and critical flow features accurately
Execute aerodynamic shape optimization studies using adjoint methods or surrogate models to minimize drag or maximize L/D for mission-critical configurations
Collaborate with structures and GNC engineers to provide aerodynamic loads and pressure distributions for structural sizing and flight control law design
Perform uncertainty quantification and CFD V&V activities to bound aerodynamic prediction errors and support flight certification with regulatory authorities
Present aerodynamic analysis results at design reviews and author detailed technical reports documenting methodology, assumptions, and engineering conclusions
